question.text: Which of the following combinations of moneyness is CORRECT for options with strike price Rs. 500 when the underlying stock trades at Rs. 480?

answer.text: Call is OTM and Put is ITM
Call is ITM and Put is OTM
Both call and put are OTM
Both call and put are ATM

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Spot (480) < Strike (500): - Call option: OTM because S (480) < X (500) - no point exercising right to buy at 500 when market is 480 - Put option: ITM because S (480) < X (500) - profitable to exercise right to sell at 500 when market is only 480
**The Trap:** Call and put moneyness are always OPPOSITE for the same strike (except ATM).
**Cross-Exam Context:** This inverse relationship is key for understanding option strategies and put-call parity.
**Key Takeaway:** If S < X: Call = OTM, Put = ITM. If S > X: Call = ITM, Put = OTM.
